ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general fruit juice class; no specific 'less sugar packaged' subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers fruit juices broadly; no dedicated less-sugar packaged subcode exists. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged less-sugar fruit juice; null applied to avoid fabrication.

main_ingredients: permitted sweeteners or reduced sugar formulation

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve portion is one 250 ml carton or bottle, equivalent to approximately 250 g, as commonly sold in Singapore convenience stores and supermarkets.

nutrient_source.title: Fruit juice (less sugar, packaged, any flavour) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Fruit juice (less sugar, packaged, any flavour)' (cr_id D03040012). All per-100g values are as reported by HPB.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fruit juice category
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 55 applied based on the University of Sydney GI Database reference range for packaged fruit juices (apple, orange, mixed fruit) with reduced sugar content. Per the University of Sydney GI classification, GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI category (low: ≤55). gi_category is therefore set to 'low'. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as (55 × 14.0 × 2.5) / 100 = 19.25, rounded to 19.3. The input record cited GI 55 with confidence 0.351; this is retained as an analogous estimate consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database range for similar products.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (11.6 g/100 g) in a liquid beverage independently raises blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 6 mg/100 g but does not offset the sugar-driven cardiovascular risk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage: despite 'less sugar' labelling, 11.6 g sugar/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold and liquid sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; plain water or unsweetened beverages are strongly preferred.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 11.6 g/100 g with zero dietary fibre in liquid form causes rapid postprandial glucose spikes; GI of 55 with a glycemic load of 19.3 per 250 ml serving is clinically significant for glycaemic management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage: the 11.6 g/100 g sugar with no fibre buffer delivers a glycemic load of approximately 19 per standard serving, causing rapid blood glucose elevation inconsistent with ADA dietary targets; whole fruit with intact fibre or plain water is recommended instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sugar content (11.6 g/100 g) with zero saturated fat or sodium concern; regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides, weight gain, and increased CVD risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit or avoid this beverage: while sodium and saturated fat are negligible, the 11.6 g/100 g sugar content in liquid form contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia and excess caloric intake, both of which are independent CVD risk factors per ACC/AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fruit Juice, Less Sugar, Packaged (Any Flavour) is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink packaged beverage sold in Singapore, catalogued under the Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo database (cr_id D03040012). Known in Chinese as 少糖包装果汁, in Malay as jus buah-buahan kurang gula, in Tamil as குறைந்த சர்க்கரை பழச்சாறு, and in Hindi as कम चीनी पैकेज्ड फलों का रस, it is available in a range of fruit flavours including apple, orange, mixed fruit, and tropical blends, and is marketed as a reduced-sugar alternative to standard packaged fruit juices. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the product contains 11.6 g of sugar per 100 g and 14.0 g of total carbohydrate per 100 g, with zero dietary fibre. Energy content is 57 kcal per 100 g. Protein is minimal at 0.1 g/100 g, and fat is absent (0.0 g/100 g, confirmed zero by product formulation as this is a carbohydrate-based liquid beverage). Sodium is low at 6 mg/100 g. The beverage is plant-based, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Clinically, this product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) because its sugar content exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low, at the upper boundary of the low GI category per University of Sydney classification), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 19 per standard 250 ml serving — a clinically significant load for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The absence of fibre means there is no buffer to slow glucose absorption. For hypertension, the AHA 2018 guidelines identify liquid sugar as an independent blood pressure risk factor regardless of low sodium. For cardiovascular disease, regular SSB consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides and excess caloric intake. This product is not suitable for individuals with diabetes, hypertension, or those actively managing cardiovascular risk. Whole fruit with intact fibre or plain water is the recommended alternative.
common_user_queries: Is less sugar fruit juice healthy?
Can diabetics drink packaged fruit juice?
Is less sugar juice good for high blood pressure?
How much sugar is in packaged fruit juice?
Is fruit juice with less sugar suitable for weight loss?
What is the glycemic index of packaged fruit juice?
Is less sugar fruit juice better than regular juice?
Can I drink fruit juice if I have diabetes?
少糖果汁适合糖尿病患者吗？
Bolehkah penghidap diabetes minum jus buah kurang gula?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D03040012) with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. Source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food products. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database range for similar packaged fruit juices. Local-language names provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this product is catalogued by the Singapore HPB and is widely consumed in Singapore's multilingual context.
notes: Fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by product formulation; this is a carbohydrate-based liquid beverage containing no fat by composition as reported by HPB. Saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero; packaged fruit juice without pulp contains no measurable dietary fibre per HPB data. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage. Calcium_mg and iron_mg are 0.0 as reported by HPB for this product. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this generic packaged fruit juice category as of 2026. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database range for packaged fruit juices; GI 55 is classified as low (≤55) per University of Sydney GI classification, hence gi_category is 'low'. Direct GI testing data for this specific HPB-catalogued product is not available in the published literature. This is a generic record covering any flavour of less-sugar packaged fruit juice; actual nutrient values may vary slightly by brand and flavour. The 'less sugar' designation is a Singapore HPB Healthier Choice Symbol category; the product still qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage by international SSB definitions (sugar > 5 g/100 g). Local-language names in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi have been added to reflect the multilingual Singapore context in which this HPB-catalogued product is consumed and searched.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name is precise and unambiguous. Local-language names now provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to reflect the Singapore HPB context. Food identity is clear and well-defined across all required languages.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (57.0), protein_g (0.1), fat_g (0.0), carbohydrate_g (14.0) all present and numeric. Source is HPB Singapore Foodo (approved primary regional source). Zero-macro justification provided in notes for fat_g. nutrient_source fully populated.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (6.0), saturated_fat_g (0.0), sugar_g (11.6) all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and consistent with HPB source data.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (55), gi_category ('low' — GI 55 is at the upper boundary of the low category per University of Sydney classification where low is ≤55), glycemic_load_per_serving (19.3), and gi_source all present. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database as analogous estimate. Estimation basis documented. gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to match GI 55 per University of Sydney classification.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI and label provided as closest_parent match with gap note. FoodEx2 code A00LC (Fruit juices) provided as closest_parent match with gap note. SNOMED CT null with documented gap note. No codes invented; all are defensible.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters (approximately 1,800 characters). Covers what the food is, multilingual names, commercial context, preparation, nutritional profile, clinical relevance for diabetes/hypertension/CVD, and recommended alternatives. Suitable for RAG and LLM training use.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, human_condition_name correctly set), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Machine key type2_diabetes used correctly.
